'''Matt Crowther''' (born 6 May 1974) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s for the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]], [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield-Sheffield Giants]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. He is the Head Physio at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Crowther started his professional career with the Sheffield Eagles. Matt Crowther played on the {{rlp|wg}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998. Following the club's merger with Huddersfield, he played for the [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield-Sheffield Giants]] during [[Super League V]] before moving to [[Hull F.C.]] in September 2000.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull snap up Crowther|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/932584.stm|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=30 September 2014|date=19 September 2000}}</ref> He remained at Hull until he was forced to retire in 2003 after breaking his leg in a match against the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Injured Crowther to retire|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3211200.stm|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=30 September 2014|date=21 October 2003}}</ref>

===International career===
Crowther was a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international and played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Post-playing career==
After retiring as a player, Crowther became a physiotherapist. After working for the [[NHS]] for several years, he returned to rugby league as a physio for [[Featherstone Rovers]]. In 2014, he became a physio at [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Crowther working hard to get Tigers fit for Cup |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/crowther-working-hard-to-get-tigers-fit-for-cup-293770 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=18 August 2023 |date=8 May 2018}}</ref>
